mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 10-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising industrial production and falling housing starts indicates a rotation from cyclical demand to defensive supply constraints, which historically precedes equity drawdowns as corporate margins compress over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO up, HOUST down) fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained period of negative real interest rates, defined by CPIAUCSL rising faster than FEDFUNDS, compresses equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The spread (CPIAUCSL - FEDFUNDS) fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly new housing starts fall below 900 thousand units, it signals a sharp contraction in consumer credit and construction demand, which historically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as growth expectations adjust.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 10-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls above 150 thousand reinforces labor market resilience, supporting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strong payroll months are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in industrial production and decline in real savings (RSAFS) indicates that economic growth is being financed by dissaving rather than productive investment, which historically precedes equity corrections and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO up, RSAFS down) fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating real GDP growth above 3.0% annualized, corporate earnings momentum dominates valuation concerns, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days regardless of short-term interest rate fluctuations.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.
